hook_check._path_mandate_note: OBJ-04 fix: Path B removed from M1 in iteration 2. AC-01 verifies Path A static-read only — EVENT_DELTAS dict values are summed directly. Eliminates Path A/B drift between spec and AC verification.

scenarios_to_verify_primary._status: PRIMARY — this is the authoritative cycle-total check
scenarios_to_verify_primary.rule: For each scenario, compute (sum over events e in scenario, sum over agents a in {dev,ba,qa}, EVENT_DELTAS[e][a]) and assert <= 5.

per_event_positive_sum_sweep_guard._status: GUARD — additional safety check, NOT a substitute for the cycle-scenario check above
per_event_positive_sum_sweep_guard.rule: For EVERY event E in EVENT_DELTAS where ANY agent has a positive value, the sum (dev_value + ba_value + qa_value) MUST be <= 5
per_event_positive_sum_sweep_guard.rationale_corrected: Per-event positive cap is a GUARD against stray non-zero values, NOT a guarantee of cycle-total cap (codex finding #6: capping each of N events at +5 individually only bounds cycle-total at N*5, not 5). The cycle-scenario check above is the authoritative gate.

hook_check._negative_deltas_full_note: OBJ-07 fix: prior iteration enumerated 7 entries; this is the full 20-entry snapshot (all entries with value < 0 across the EVENT_DELTAS dict at git HEAD pre-iter-2-dev-edits). For every event in this list, the post-change value MUST equal the listed value exactly. Source-of-truth snapshot is scripts/score-update.sh EVENT_DELTAS at git HEAD prior to dev's M1 edits (commit-pinned via pre-change Read captured in dev-report).

stale_strings_to_grep_out._note: qa_first_pass parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value (dev:0, ba:0, qa:0); old `(dev +6, ba +3, qa 0.)` text must be replaced
OBJ-06 addition: qa_second_pass parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value (dev:0); old `(dev +3.)` text must be replaced
close_success_qa_pass parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value; old `(dev +5, ba +3, qa +3.)` text must be replaced
OBJ-06 addition: close_success_qa_fail_fixed parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value; old `(dev +5, ba +3, qa +2.)` text must be replaced

hook_check._objection_rationale: User requirement R2 verbatim: '验证 agent 在被注入后真的会读 + 行动' (verify agent reads AND acts after injection). Iter 1 contract only required rank_acknowledged + range_acknowledged — both low-entropy (5 buckets each) and mechanically copy-able from the injected header, proving at most 'read' (or just 'echo'). Iter 2 adds (i) recent_events_digest_acknowledged (sha256 8-char prefix over DYNAMIC content — forces the agent to process the payload, not just echo labels) and (ii) score_injection_action (non-empty free-text — forces the agent to articulate a behavioral consequence, proving '行动' / act).
hook_check.agent_contract_failsafe_required: set ALL FOUR fields to null when injection absent/malformed; do not fabricate

recent_events_digest_spec.input: the literal text of the recent-events section emitted by score-inject for this run (the bulleted history block — whatever score-inject prints as 'Recent events:' content)
recent_events_digest_spec.output_format: first 8 hex chars of the lowercase hex digest
recent_events_digest_spec.rationale: High-entropy 32-bit fingerprint computed over DYNAMIC content (recent events differ run-to-run) — proves the agent processed the injection payload.

score_injection_action_spec.constraint: free-text 1-line description that MUST (i) cite at least ONE concrete signal from the injection (a specific rank label, range bucket, or named recent-event verbatim or paraphrased), AND (ii) cite at least ONE concrete behavioral adjustment the agent took or will take this run
score_injection_action_spec.rationale: Free-text floor proves 'act' (行动). Iter-2-post-codex (codex #1 finding): raw 10-char min was gameable via 'no action needed'. Iter-2-post-codex tightens: (a) length floor 20 chars; (b) must reference a concrete injection signal AND a concrete behavior adjustment; (c) reject placeholder values verbatim. The runtime verifier in tests/score-inject-contract/runtime-verify.sh MUST: (1) extract score_injection_action; (2) lowercase + strip whitespace; (3) reject if it matches (== or is wholly contained by trimming punctuation) any forbidden_placeholder_values; (4) emit a 'low quality action description' warning if it does not contain any of the actual rank label / range string / a recent-event keyword from the injected text. The warning is informational; the hard fail is the placeholder rejection + length floor.

recent_events_digest_recomputation_requi.rule: The test fixture runner (test-inject-branches.sh) AND the runtime verifier (runtime-verify.sh) MUST INDEPENDENTLY parse the actual recent-events text emitted by score-inject.sh in each branch, recompute sha256(text).hexdigest()[:8], and assert the substituted <RECENT_EVENTS_DIGEST> in INJECTION_PROOF EQUALS the recomputed value. This prevents the script-side bug where score-inject substitutes a random or stale 8-hex string that happens to be valid hex but is not actually derived from the recent-events text.
recent_events_digest_recomputation_requi.test_implementation_sketch: In test-inject-branches.sh: capture full stdout of score-inject.sh; extract the 'Recent events:' block (or whatever label the script emits) via a known delimiter or section header; pipe through `sha256sum | cut -c1-8`; assert equals the digest cited on the INJECTION_PROOF line.
recent_events_digest_recomputation_requi.runtime_verifier_implementation_sketch: In runtime-verify.sh: read the captured score-inject output snapshot (saved at dispatch time per QA contract), extract recent-events text, recompute digest, assert equals .recent_events_digest_acknowledged jq-extracted from the qa-report.

runtime_artifact_to_jq_verify.match_against: captured score-inject output from QA dispatch this cycle; rank/range/digest must match exactly; score_injection_action must be non-null and length >= 10

hook_check._forbidden_phrases_note: These six phrases (verbatim from current close.md:425-429) must NOT appear as the template's required content directives. They may still appear elsewhere in close.md as historical context if the dev keeps the old text as a removed-block reference.

hook_check._purpose_note: OBJ-03 fix: iter-1 BA claimed R7 (spec-continue trigger) was implicit via AC-04. QA-iter1 confirmed by direct read of commit.md:176-181 that Step 7 SKIPS when BULK=true. This AC verifies the full chain: blocked-bulk → non-bulk fallback → Step 7 fires.

static_assertions_about_step_7._context: Step 7 SKIP condition must include BULK=true (existing behavior; asserting unchanged and observable)
Step 7 dispatch logic is present and not accidentally removed by M4 edits

preconditions: fresh test git repo with a dirty tree (uncommitted file)
runtime_test_phase_a_blocked_bulk.exec_simulation: Invoke the bulk-commit-sentinel write path (the script directly OR via a /commit --bulk simulation; orchestrator runs from non-subagent context per OBJ-02 split)
assertions: no file matching the push-gate token path was created
no spec-update marker emitted to stderr / trace log

preconditions: same dirty tree from Phase A still uncommitted (in a SYNTHETIC test git repo — not the project working tree)
test-mode env var COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1 OR dev added an observable step7 trace mechanism per (c)(i)
TASK_ID set to the synthetic task-id
runtime_test_phase_b_non_bulk_fallback.exec_real_fallback_chain_required: Invoke /commit <task-id> (non-bulk path) END-TO-END on the synthetic dirty test repo. Iter-2-post-codex (codex finding #3): the stubbed Phase B option is REMOVED. The test MUST run the real commit.md Step 5/6/7 sequence — real changelog-analyst dispatch, real commit, real push-gate token write, real Step 7 dispatch evaluation. The synthetic repo isolates this from the live working tree.
runtime_test_phase_b_non_bulk_fallback.supplemental_unit_test_allowed: Dev MAY ADDITIONALLY include a unit test that exercises Step 7 logic in isolation with a stub push-gate token. This is SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY — it does NOT substitute for the real end-to-end Phase B test. The dev-report MUST clearly label the unit test as supplemental and the e2e test as primary.
assertions: Real commit SHA changed (new commit object in synthetic repo's git log)
changelog-analyst returned commit_status=committed (per its JSON contract)
Push-gate token written at the expected path
Step 7 SKIP did NOT fire (BULK=false, DRYRUN=false, TASK_ID present, push-gate token present)

deferral_clause._purpose: AC-05 may NOT be self-deferred by dev. Iter-2-post-codex tightens (codex finding #4): deferral requires a SEPARATE, CITABLE user message dated AFTER this ba-spec's `revised_at` timestamp that explicitly approves AC-05 deferral. The dev-report MUST cite the user message verbatim (or a path to the artifact storing it) AND QA MUST refuse to accept the deferral if that artifact is absent OR if the cited message does not literally approve AC-05 deferral.

schema.user_message_verbatim: string (the user's exact words approving deferral)
schema.user_message_artifact_path: string (path to the file containing the message, e.g. docs/dev/user-clarifications-20260524-205206.md)
schema.claude_session_id: string (the session in which the user provided consent)

deferral_evidence_required.qa_validation_rule: QA MUST grep the cited artifact path for the verbatim user_message string; QA MUST verify the timestamp is AFTER revision_metadata.revised_at; if either check fails, QA MUST mark AC-05 as fail (NOT deferred).

archived_tests.retired_reason: Obsolete: AC-04 tested the bulk-commit auth-flag mechanism (/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-<sid>.flag, auth_flag_path, CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE, BULK_AUTH_FLAG, unlink-first ordering) which was REMOVED in cycle 20260526-052559 (Cycle 8). The replacement is the sentinel file /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-<sid>-<nonce>.json written by scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py; the slash command's disable-model-invocation:true is the authorization (no auth flag). The _static_assertions() asserted removed strings present in source files and can never pass again; the runtime cases targeting /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-* are no longer guarded by the hooks. Test file deleted; assertions NOT rewritten to the sentinel path (that would fabricate a never-specified test).
archived_tests.coverage_escalation: g_hook_3 (Write tool -> /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json DENY via hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh) and the analogous NotebookEdit->sentinel.json case describe CURRENT, still-active protection in pretool-write-guard.sh (lines 13-24) that is NOT covered by the Bash-only 20260526-053746 suite nor by 20260522-080646-B (which tests the unrelated /tmp/claude-grants sentinel-grant system). Retiring this file drops the only Write/NotebookEdit-channel coverage of the bulk-commit sentinel write-guard. Escalated for the orchestrator to add a focused replacement test if desired.
